Our send-and-leave verb works because it uses ChatCreateSession, but the Leaving field we added to ChatSendMessageRequest in Phase A does nothing. Either remove it from the client type or ask grace to add server support.

title: Headless bootstrap doubles /api prefix when base URL already includes /api
description:
Found by Wendy. resolveWorkingBaseURL() in cmd/aw/helpers.go:203-215 can resolve to a base URL ending in /api (e.g. https://host.example/api). The headless bootstrap client method then posts to /api/v1/bootstrap/headless-agent relative to that base, producing https://host.example/api/api/v1/bootstrap/headless-agent.

Same issue affects cloudRootBaseURL() in cmd/aw/init.go:673-685 which only strips an exact trailing /api, not custom mounts like /custom/api.

Existing test coverage proves custom mounts matter (TestAwInitAllowsCustomMountRoot expects /custom/v1/init). But no equivalent test for headless or cloud bootstrap with /api-suffixed base URLs.

Fix direction: Normalize bootstrap endpoints the same way the rest of the client resolves mounted base URLs. The headless and cloud bootstrap paths should use the same base URL as /v1/init, not assume host root.

Tests needed:
- init against server resolved to /api with headless bootstrap
- init cloud mode against /custom/api
- custom mount + headless fallback behavior
close_reason: Fixed /api prefix doubling: tryHeadlessOrInit now creates separate client with cloudRootBaseURL to strip /api suffix. Also generalized cloudRootBaseURL to handle custom mount paths. Code reviewed.

title: Replace fragile mockHandler prefix matching in chat tests
description:
The mockHandler in chat_test.go uses map iteration (undefined order) for prefix matching of parameterized paths. This is fragile — if two handlers share a prefix, behavior is non-deterministic.

Several tests already work around this with explicit path guards:
  if r.URL.Path != "/v1/chat/sessions" { http.NotFound(w, r); return }

This pattern appears in TestOpenFallbackToListSessions, TestFindSessionFallback, TestFindSessionNotFound, TestFindSessionPrefersSmallestFallback, TestListenNoSession.

Fix: Replace prefix matching with proper path routing. Options:
- Use http.ServeMux (Go 1.22+ supports method+path patterns)
- Use exact matching only, register parameterized paths explicitly per test
- Use a simple trie or ordered slice instead of a map

Files: chat/chat_test.go
close_reason: Removed prefix matching from mockHandler, using exact method+path matching only. Removed 5 path-checking workarounds. All 37 tests pass.

title: State-aware input prompt that communicates system phase
description: The input prompt shows the same label regardless of what the system is doing. The user can't tell if the agent is running (type to queue), paused (type to resume), waiting for work events (nothing to do), or in an idle countdown (about to start). Change the prompt label or style to reflect the current phase. Consider: dimming/hiding the prompt during active runs, showing 'paused>' during pause, showing 'queued>' when a prompt override is pending. Key files: run/loop.go (renderInputPrompt), run/control.go (FormatInputLine).
close_reason: Status bar now shows 'queued' indicator when user has submitted a prompt during an active run. With the persistent status bar (aw-qhi), system phase is now communicated through the status bar rather than the prompt label.

title: Library: ScanKeysForDID helper
description: Add awconfig.ScanKeysForDID(keysDir, targetDID string) (privKeyPath string, err error). Algorithm: (1) list all .signing.key files in keysDir, (2) for each, load private key, derive public key, compute did:key, (3) if match: return path. Also checks keysDir/rotated/ for archived keys. Returns empty string + nil if not found. This enables the 409 recovery flow: resolve server DID, then scan local keys for a matching private key. Unit tests with real key files.
close_reason: ScanKeysForPublicKey implemented with 6 tests, all passing

title: Network chat send-and-wait doesn't wait (no SSE streaming)
description: The network path for send-and-wait (org/alias addresses) calls NetworkCreateChat and returns immediately without opening an SSE stream. The server returns sse_url in the response but the client never connects to it. This means cross-org send-and-wait is actually fire-and-forget, violating the protocol rule: always wait unless the interlocutor left. The fix is to connect to the returned sse_url and wait for a reply, same as the OSS path does via chat.Send(). This would also fix the output schema inconsistency (network returns NetworkChatCreateResponse, OSS returns SendResult).
notes: Ivy confirmed (aweb-cloud): SSE streaming works for network chat via /api/v1/network/chat/{session_id}/stream (cloud endpoint). The sse_url in NetworkChatCreateResponse already points there. Client needs same auth + deadline query param. Fix: after NetworkCreateChat(), open SSE stream at server_url+sse_url and wait, same as OSS path.
close_reason: SendNetwork() added with full SSE streaming. Both OSS and network paths share sendCommon() waiting logic. Output schema unified to SendResult. All tests pass.

title: SOT mismatch: VerifiedCustodial never assigned on receive
description: SOT §7 step 9 says 'Check custody: Custodial → VerifiedCustodial.' But VerifyMessage() always returns Verified (never VerifiedCustodial). The sender's custody model is not available on the receive side without a network call. The distinction is informational only (lets callers treat custodial signatures differently), so severity is low. Same resolution options as the lifetime issue: resolve on receive, server includes in message metadata, or document as Phase 1 limitation.
close_reason: Fixed: CheckTOFUPin returns VerifiedCustodial when resolver reports custody=custodial.

title: SSE error events silently dropped by EventBus
description:
Found by Wendy. EventBus.consumeStream() in run/eventbus.go:269-274 drops AgentEventError before it reaches the queue or the loop. Server-originated SSE error events no longer wake the loop or surface to the operator.

This is a regression from the old wake-stream behavior where stream errors were explicitly surfaced and could affect waiting behavior. It also creates inconsistency: aw events stream prints error events, but aw run silently ignores them.

The loop still has explicit handling for ControlStreamError (run/loop.go:783-789) and the classification code still expects error events to exist (awid/event_source.go:18-22, 40-55). Current tests encode the drop behavior rather than validating the earlier intent.

Fix direction: Decide whether SSE error events should be informational-but-visible or wake-worthy. Either is defensible, but silently dropping while retaining downstream handling is inconsistent. If visible, queue or forward AgentEventError so the loop can surface it. Add test proving SSE error event produces expected behavior in aw run.
close_reason: SSE error events now surfaced via onError callback on EventBus. Loop prints 'info: event stream error: <text>'. Error events remain informational (not queued). Test race-free. Code reviewed.

title: Arrow keys should navigate within textarea, not scroll viewport
description: In screen.go:518, tea.KeyUp and tea.KeyDown are unconditionally routed to the viewport for scrolling output. They never reach the textarea. This means: (1) users cannot move the cursor up/down in multi-line input, (2) pressing arrow-up does not recall or navigate text as expected. Fix: when the textarea has focus and contains multi-line content (or any content), arrow up/down should navigate within the textarea. Viewport scrolling should use PgUp/PgDown or a modifier key (e.g. Ctrl+Up/Down, or only scroll when textarea is empty). Key file: run/screen.go lines 518-522 (tea.KeyMsg handler).
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace chat send with send-and-wait / send-and-leave verbs
description:
## Problem

Agents get trapped in 120s waits because the default chat send behavior is ambiguous. When replying to a message, agents forget --leave-conversation and silently wait for a counter-reply that never comes.

## Solution

Replace the ambiguous chat send with two explicit verbs:

- chat send-and-wait <alias> <msg> — send and wait for reply (120s default)
  - --start-conversation: 5 min wait (initiating new exchange)
  - --wait N: custom wait duration
- chat send-and-leave <alias> <msg> — send with leaving=true, exit immediately

Remove bare chat send entirely. Every send must declare intent.

## Files to update

### aw repo (this repo)
- cmd/aw/chat.go: replace chatSendCmd with two new commands
- cmd/aw/aw_test.go: update any chat send tests
- README.md: update CLI docs
- skills/aweb/SKILL.md: update command list
- skills/aweb/resources/COORDINATION_PATTERNS.md: update examples

### bdh repo (alice-coordinator)
- internal/commands/chat.go: replace send subcommand with two verbs
- internal/commands/chat_test.go: update tests
- internal/commands/agent_docs.go: update help templates
- README.md

### beadhub repo (alice-coordinator)
- src/beadhub/defaults/invariants/03-communication-chat.md: update subcommand table and examples
- src/beadhub/defaults/roles/backend.md, frontend.md: update examples

### aweb-cloud repo (ivy-implementer)
- .release-deps/beadhub/: will get updated on next beadhub release

### beadhub-sot
- archive/beadhub-chat-spec.md: update CLI section

title: Hosted alias-collision retry falls through to /v1/init
description: Quinn finding #1. When aw init on hosted uses headless bootstrap and the suggested alias collides (resp.Created=false), the retry clears req.Alias and calls tryHeadlessOrInit again. With empty alias, tryHeadlessOrInit falls to /v1/init which 404s on hosted, producing 'server requires an alias'. The hosted frictionless path fails if the suggested alias is already taken. Fix: on hosted, the retry with empty alias should not happen — the headless endpoint always requires alias. Either skip the retry for headless (the server returns 409 for conflicts, not created=false) or generate a new alias locally.
close_reason: All three Quinn review findings fixed: alias retry skips headless, ServerURL propagated from cloud bootstrap, AWEB_NAMESPACE exports slug not domain. Code reviewed.

title: SOT mismatch: CheckTOFUPin hardcodes lifetime=persistent
description: SOT §7 step 7 says 'Check agent lifetime: If ephemeral → skip pin check.' But CheckTOFUPin (client.go:170) always passes LifetimePersistent to CheckPin, regardless of the sender's actual lifetime. The sender's lifetime is not available on the receive side without a network call to resolve the sender's identity. Impact: ephemeral senders get pinned, and a future ephemeral agent reusing the same alias triggers IdentityMismatch. Options: (a) resolve sender lifetime via ServerResolver on receive (adds latency), (b) expect the server to include lifetime in message metadata, (c) document as Phase 1 limitation in SOT.

title: Wire ClawDID stable_id for go-live (aw-side)
description: Ship the aw-side plumbing so ClawDID cross-check becomes active once aweb persists stable_id fields. Three steps: (1) default-enable ClawDIDClient in CLI so CheckTOFUPin cross-check fires when from_stable_id is present, (2) compute and store stable_id in config at register/init/connect so outgoing messages can carry it, (3) populate from_stable_id on outgoing signed messages. All safe to ship without aweb changes — the full loop closes when aweb adds stable_id columns. Tara reviewed and agreed on sequencing and custody guidance (client-computed, did:claw method, never recompute after rotate).
close_reason: All 3 sub-beads complete: (1) default-enable ClawDIDClient in CLI, (2) store stable_id in config at register/init, (3) populate from_stable_id on all outgoing signed messages. aw-side ClawDID wiring is done; aweb-side persistence/return of stable_id is the remaining dependency.

title: Default-enable ClawDIDClient in CLI
description:
Wire SetClawDIDClient() in mustResolve() so the cross-check in CheckTOFUPin fires when incoming messages carry from_stable_id.

Implementation:
- Add ClawDIDRegistryURL field to awconfig.GlobalConfig (YAML: clawdid_registry_url)
- Add ClawDIDRegistryURL to Selection, propagate in finalizeSelection
- Default value: https://api.clawdid.ai (can be overridden via config or CLAWDID_REGISTRY_URL env var)
- In mustResolve() (cmd/aw/helpers.go): if sel.ClawDIDRegistryURL is non-empty, create ClawDIDClient and call client.SetClawDIDClient()
- Allow opt-out by setting empty string in config

TDD:
- Test: config with clawdid_registry_url propagates to Selection
- Test: mustResolve() sets ClawDIDClient on client when URL present
- Test: empty URL means no ClawDIDClient set
- Run code-reviewer before closing
close_reason: Implemented and code-reviewed. ClawDIDRegistryURL flows from config/env/default into Selection, mustResolve() wires SetClawDIDClient.

title: Store stable_id in config at register/init/connect
description:
Compute and persist stable_id (did:claw) at agent creation/connection time so it's available for outgoing messages and never changes across rotations.

Implementation:
- Add StableID string field to awconfig.Account (YAML: stable_id) and Selection
- Propagate StableID in finalizeSelection
- register.go: after successful register, if self-custody (signing key present), compute stable_id from initial pubkey via ComputeStableID(pub, "claw"). If custodial, compute from server-returned did:key's pubkey (did:key is deterministic → stable_id is deterministic).
- init.go: same logic — compute from pubkey at creation time
- connect.go: compute from introspect response. If introspect returns a did:key or public_key, derive stable_id. If not available, leave empty (custodial agents may not expose pubkey via introspect).
- CRITICAL: once stored, NEVER recompute on rotate. Rotation changes the did:key but stable_id is derived from the initial key.
- Use did:claw method (matches conformance vectors and SOT)

TDD:
- Test: register with self-custody stores stable_id derived from signing key
- Test: connect stores stable_id when pubkey available
- Test: stable_id persists in config YAML correctly
- Test: Selection.StableID populated from Account
- Run code-reviewer before closing
close_reason: StableID field added to Account/Selection. Computed from initial pubkey at register/init. connect.go preserves existing identity fields on re-connect. Code-reviewed.

title: Populate from_stable_id on outgoing signed messages
description:
Set FromStableID on outgoing message envelopes when stable_id is available in config, so recipients can cross-check via ClawDID once aweb persists the field.

Implementation:
- In Client.signEnvelope() (or SendMessage/ChatCreateSession/ChatSendMessage): if the client has a stable_id configured, set envelope.FromStableID before signing
- The stable_id must be part of the canonical signed payload (it already is — CanonicalJSON includes from_stable_id when non-empty, confirmed by conformance vectors)
- Pass stable_id through from Selection → Client. Options: (a) store on Client struct at construction, (b) pass via context, (c) add to signEnvelope params. Prefer (a) for simplicity — add a StableID field to Client, set it in mustResolve() alongside ClawDIDClient.
- to_stable_id: defer until aweb resolve endpoint returns stable_id for recipients

TDD:
- Test: signed message includes from_stable_id when client has stable_id
- Test: signed message omits from_stable_id when client has no stable_id (backward compat)
- Test: canonical payload with from_stable_id matches expected format
- Test: signature verification succeeds with from_stable_id present
- Run code-reviewer before closing
close_reason: All 7 outgoing request structs now transmit from_stable_id on the wire. Tests verify signedFields carries the value and signature is valid.

title: Fix Listen to not accept replayed historical messages
description:
Listen uses an acceptAll acceptor that grabs the first SSE event it sees. Since the SSE stream replays historical messages, Listen immediately returns the oldest message in the conversation instead of waiting for new ones.

Fix: Once the server supports the 'after' parameter, Listen passes no 'after' value so the server skips replay entirely. Listen then only receives genuinely new messages.

Interim workaround (if needed before server change): snapshot the latest message ID via ChatHistory(limit=1) before opening SSE, use an acceptor that skips until past that ID.
close_reason: Implemented in commit c40f1b8: ChatStream/NetworkChatStream accept after param, Send passes sentAt timestamp, Listen passes nil, skipped replay events filtered from result.Events

title: Refactor: eliminate fatal()/os.Exit from CLI, return errors through Cobra
description:
Comprehensive error-handling refactor for the aw CLI. Replace all fatal()/os.Exit patterns with proper Go error returns through Cobra RunE. Introduces cliError type for structured exit codes. Converts must* helpers to fallible resolve* functions. Also includes SDK-level improvements: request non-mutation (copy-before-sign), verifyChatMessages DRY extraction, chat session helper unification, SSE EOF handling, and ServerResolver stable_id/public_key parsing.

Reference diff saved at /tmp/zoe-error-handling-refactor.diff (from zoe branch working tree, based on 98cdaa8).

## Implementation Plan (apply on main)

### Phase 1: Leaf dependencies (no other changes needed)
1. Add cmd/aw/errors.go — cliError type, usageError(), exitCode()
2. Add cmd/aw/heartbeat.go — explicit heartbeat subcommand

### Phase 2: SDK-level changes (independent of CLI refactor)
3. identity.go — ServerResolver parses stable_id + public_key, cross-checks DID
4. chat.go, mail.go, network.go — copy-before-sign (request non-mutation)
5. chat.go, network.go — extract verifyChatMessages helper
6. chat/chat.go — extract session helpers (openSession, historySession, etc.) to DRY local/network paths
7. chat/chat.go — SSE stream: distinguish io.EOF from real errors

### Phase 3: CLI error-return conversion
8. cmd/aw/helpers.go — remove fireHeartbeat(), probeAwebBaseURL(); simplify resolveWorkingBaseURL()
9. cmd/aw/helpers.go — rename must* to resolve* with error returns (resolveClientSelection, resolveClient, resolveAPIKeyOnly, resolveAgentID, defaultGlobalPath)
10. cmd/aw/root.go — remove go fireHeartbeat(); add checkVerificationRequired + exitCode to Execute()
11. All cmd/aw/*.go RunE handlers — replace fatal(err) with return err
12. All cmd/aw/*.go RunE handlers — replace os.Exit(2) patterns with return usageError(...)
13. Internal functions (provisionIdentity, recoverIdentity409, saveNewRegistration, etc.) — add error returns

### Phase 4: Tests
14. client_test.go — non-mutation tests for all copy-before-sign methods
15. identity_test.go — ServerResolver stable_id + public_key tests
16. network_test.go — updated test expectations
17. cmd/aw/aw_test.go — update init test for no-probing behavior

### Phase 5: Cleanup
18. Struct field alignment normalization (whitespace)
19. docs/identity-system.md — remove probing docs, note base URL is authoritative

## Key design decisions
- cliError carries exit code; usageError() returns code 2
- Execute() in root.go is the single place that maps errors to stderr + exit codes
- checkVerificationRequired hint enrichment moves from fatal() to Execute()
- resolveWorkingBaseURL no longer probes — treats configured URL as authoritative
- SDK methods shallow-copy request structs + deep-copy slices before signing
close_reason: Completed by zoe's refactor (merged commit 60e9c38, v0.25.0)

title: aw client: replacement verification and lifecycle UX
description:
Client-side work needed to complete the archive/replace lifecycle (server side done by quinn in aweb-cloud 8c52ec1).

From quinn's spec, what aw needs:
1. Teach aw verification to distinguish old-key rotation from controller-authorized replacement
2. Accept DID changes for a pinned public address only when a valid replacement_announcement is present and signed by the trusted controller_did for that address; otherwise keep identity_mismatch
3. Surface replacement as a distinct trust state in CLI/chat/mail UX, not as plain verified continuity
4. Update aw docs/help text so archive vs rotation vs replacement is explicit

Server payloads now include:
- replacement_announcement alongside from_address in mail/chat/MCP responses
- controller_did in resolver/publication payloads

Behavioral model:
- Rotation: same did:aw, new did:key, signed by old key — cryptographic continuity
- Replacement: new did:aw, new did:key, controller-authorized — address continuity only
- Archive: agent removed from active views, no successor

title: Extend pin store to handle replacement announcements
description:
Extend the TOFU pin store (awid/pinstore.go) to handle controller-authorized replacements.

SPECIFICATION:

Current behavior:
- CheckPin returns verified/first_use/identity_mismatch based on stored did:key for an address
- Key rotation (same did:aw, new did:key signed by old key) updates the pin

New behavior needed:
- When a DID change occurs for a pinned address AND a replacement_announcement is present:
  - Verify the replacement_announcement is signed by the controller_did for that address
  - If valid: accept the new DID, update the pin, return a new status: 'replaced' (not 'verified')
  - If invalid or missing: keep returning 'identity_mismatch'
- When a DID change occurs WITHOUT replacement_announcement:
  - Keep existing behavior: return 'identity_mismatch'

New CheckPin return values:
- verified — same key, normal verification
- first_use — TOFU, first time seeing this address
- identity_mismatch — key changed without valid authorization
- rotated — same did:aw, new did:key, signed rotation
- replaced — new did:aw, new did:key, controller-authorized replacement

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add controller_did to PinEntry (stored per address)
- Add CheckPinWithReplacement method that takes the replacement_announcement
- Signature verification: Ed25519 verify the announcement payload with controller_did's public key
- Store the controller_did from resolver/publication responses when first pinning

FILES:
- awid/pinstore.go
- awid/pinstore_test.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Wire replacement verification into message reception
description:
Integrate replacement-aware verification into the mail and chat message reception paths.

SPECIFICATION:
- When receiving a mail or chat message with a from_address:
  1. Resolve the sender's current identity (did:key, did:aw, controller_did)
  2. Verify the message signature against the sender's current did:key
  3. Check the pin store for the from_address
  4. If the pin shows identity_mismatch AND replacement_announcement is present:
     - Call CheckPinWithReplacement to verify the controller signature
     - If valid: accept the message, mark trust state as 'replaced'
  5. If identity_mismatch with no replacement_announcement: reject/warn as before

- The verification result must carry the trust distinction:
  - verified: normal cryptographic continuity
  - replaced: controller-authorized address reassignment (not cryptographic continuity)
  - unverified: no signature or verification failed

FILES:
- chat/chat.go — chat message reception
- cmd/aw/chat.go — chat display
- cmd/aw/mail.go — mail display
- awid/signing.go — signature verification
close_reason: Closed

title: Surface replacement trust state in CLI UX
description:
Display the replacement trust state distinctly in CLI output for chat, mail, and introspect commands.

SPECIFICATION:

Chat display:
- Verified messages: show as normal (no badge)
- Replaced messages: show '[replaced]' badge — the address is the same but the agent behind it changed via controller authorization
- Unverified: show '[unverified]' as before

Mail display:
- Same trust badges in aw mail inbox output

Introspect / whoami:
- If the current agent was created via replacement, show in aw whoami output:
  'Replaces: <old_did_aw> (controller-authorized)'

Help text:
- aw did log should show replacement events in the audit trail
- aw agent retire help text should mention archive as the preferred lifecycle action

The key UX principle: replacement is NOT the same as rotation. Rotation is cryptographic continuity (the old key vouched for the new one). Replacement is organizational continuity (the controller reassigned the address). Users and agents must be able to tell the difference.

FILES:
- cmd/aw/chat.go — chat message display
- cmd/aw/mail.go — mail display
- cmd/aw/introspect.go — whoami output
- cmd/aw/did.go — did log display

title: CLI: create identity-capable client when signing key present
description:
mustResolve() in cmd/aw/helpers.go always builds the client with NewWithAPIKey(baseURL, sel.APIKey) and never uses sel.SigningKey/sel.DID to construct NewWithIdentity. Result: outbound mail/chat/network sends don't carry client signatures even for self-custody accounts. Fix: when sel.SigningKey and sel.DID are both present, use NewWithIdentity(baseURL, sel.APIKey, sel.SigningKey, sel.DID) instead of NewWithAPIKey. Fall back to NewWithAPIKey when identity fields are absent (legacy/custodial accounts).

title: Auto-compact should not count toward max-runs
description: When context exceeds the threshold, the loop inserts an extra /compact run that counts toward maxRuns. This means --max-runs=5 might only give 3-4 real runs. The user sees run numbers jump (run #3 to run #5) with no explanation. Fix: either don't increment state.Run for compact runs, or track compact runs separately and exclude them from the maxRuns check. Also make the compact run visually distinct (e.g. 'compact #1' instead of 'run #5'). Key files: run/loop.go (maybeAutoCompact, Run loop around line 158-164).

title: CLI: set client address for signed envelopes
description:
client.go signEnvelope() sets env.From = c.address, but c.address is never set by production CLI code. SetAddress() exists but is only called in tests (client_test.go). Result: even once we enable identity-capable client creation (aw-9g9), the From field in signed envelopes will be empty.

Fix: after creating the client with NewWithIdentity in mustResolve/mustClient, call c.SetAddress() with the agent's canonical address derived from the selection (namespace/alias or project/alias). The address is available from sel fields.

Two options:
a) Add address parameter to NewWithIdentity
b) Call SetAddress() in mustResolve() after client creation

Option (b) is simpler and matches the existing API surface.

Files: cmd/aw/helpers.go
Depends on: aw-9g9
Ref: Tara's SOT audit finding #2
close_reason: Fixed in mustResolve: identity client + SetAddress when signing key present

title: fix: self-custody mail signs wrong to field
description: Self-custody mail signing uses raw alias (e.g. 'monitor') as the 'to' field in the signed envelope, but inbox verification reconstructs 'to' from ToAddress which is the canonical address (e.g. 'myco/monitor'). This causes signature verification failure. Fix: when signingKey \!= nil and ToAlias is a plain alias, sign with namespaceSlug()/ToAlias. Mirrors chat fix in e8f7128.

title: retire.go and did.go bypass URL probing when creating identity client
description:
retire.go (line 50) and did.go (line 72) construct NewWithIdentity clients using raw sel.BaseURL without going through resolveWorkingBaseURL(). This means key rotation and agent retirement bypass the URL-probing logic that mustResolve() performs (trying /api suffix, stripping /v1, etc). If the user's configured URL needs probing, these commands will hit the wrong endpoint.

These paths predate the mustResolve() identity fix and were written before mustResolve() handled identity. Now that mustResolve() does the right thing, these should be refactored to use mustResolve() instead of duplicating client creation.

Files: cmd/aw/retire.go:50, cmd/aw/did.go:72
Ref: Code review finding on aw-9g9/aw-am0
close_reason: retire.go and did.go now use mustResolve() client instead of bypassing URL probing with raw sel.BaseURL

title: Pass sent message timestamp as after in sendCommon SSE connect
description:
sendCommon opens SSE to wait for replies. Currently the SSE stream replays up to 50 historical messages and the acceptor skips past the sent message ID. This wastes bandwidth and latency.

Fix: Pass the sent message's timestamp as the 'after' parameter when opening the SSE stream. The server will only replay messages since the send (usually 0-1, catching the send→SSE race window). The sentMessageID skip logic in the acceptor can be simplified or removed since replayed messages before the send won't appear.

Depends on: ChatStream after parameter being added.
close_reason: Implemented in commit c40f1b8: ChatStream/NetworkChatStream accept after param, Send passes sentAt timestamp, Listen passes nil, skipped replay events filtered from result.Events

title: Adopt actionable coordination wake events in aw client

Client work includes event schema support, wake_mode-aware prioritization in the runtime loop, operator-facing prompt/output changes, backward compatibility with legacy event names during rollout, and regression coverage for reconnect/urgent-wake behavior.
acceptance_criteria: aw can consume actionable_chat and actionable_mail events alongside legacy mail_message/chat_message during rollout; wake_mode is surfaced to runtime policy without conflating wake with direct control; aw run reacts correctly to idle/prompt/interrupt urgency; operator-facing event inspection and run prompts reflect the new semantics; regression tests cover parser compatibility, queue/interrupt routing, and urgent wake behavior

Extend awid.AgentEventType and AgentEvent payload decoding to cover actionable_chat and actionable_mail plus wake metadata needed by the runtime and operator tooling. The parser should treat these as state-based wake notifications rather than historical message rows.

title: Teach run/eventbus and loop to route actionable wake urgency correctly

Prompt and idle wakes should continue through the normal wake path. Interrupt-grade actionable wakes must be able to break an active provider run when local runtime policy allows, while remaining distinct from direct control_interrupt/pause/resume semantics. Preserve backward compatibility with legacy event names and existing control behavior.
acceptance_criteria: eventbus classifies actionable wake events by urgency; wake_mode=idle|prompt follows the deferred wake path; wake_mode=interrupt can trigger an urgent local wake during an active run without being parsed as a direct control signal; paused/idle/runtime states behave coherently when urgent wakes arrive; legacy control_pause/control_resume/control_interrupt semantics remain unchanged and covered by tests

Coverage should prove both compatibility with legacy event names and correctness for actionable_chat/actionable_mail, especially the urgent case where wake_mode=interrupt must preempt the current run without being mistaken for a direct control signal.

title: Stale wake events dispatched after hook-handled chat

If the chat has been read or mail acknowledged during the run, skip the dispatch. Key files: run/loop.go (dispatch logic after run), cmd/aw/run_dispatch.go.

title: Wire TOFU pin store into inbox/chat receive verification
description:
Verification currently only checks signature vs from_did (signing.go VerifyMessage). No TOFU pin store integration in inbox/chat receive paths — mail.go Inbox and chat.go ChatHistory just call VerifyMessage and stamp the status.

SOT requires on receive:
- First contact from a DID: create TOFU pin (from_did -> public key) in local pin store
- Subsequent contact: verify from_did matches pinned key
- Lifetime-aware behavior: skip TOFU for ephemeral agents (lifetime=ephemeral)
- Identity mismatch on pinned agent: attempt rotation_announcement validation before warning/quarantine

pinstore.go already exists with Pin struct (has StableID field). Need to:
1. Call pin store during inbox/chat message verification
2. Create pin on first verified contact
3. Check pin on subsequent contacts
4. Integrate lifetime from agent config (skip for ephemeral)
5. Return IdentityMismatch status when pin doesn't match and no valid rotation

Files: mail.go (Inbox), chat.go (ChatHistory), network.go (NetworkChatHistory), chat/chat.go (parseSSEEvent), pinstore.go
Depends on: aw-9g9, aw-am0
Ref: Tara's SOT audit finding #4
close_reason: Wired TOFU pin store into all receive paths (inbox, chat history, network chat history, SSE). Thread-safe with mutex. CLI loads from ~/.config/aw/pins.yaml.

title: Prune ephemeral identity pins instead of only ignoring them
description: Harden TOFU correctness for ephemeral identities. Today ephemeral senders are skipped for continuity checks, but stale pins can still remain in known_agents.yaml and identity delete does not remove them. Update the pin store/model so ephemeral identities do not leave continuity state behind: prune matching pins on delete, and opportunistically clear stale pins when a sender resolves as ephemeral. Add regressions for reused ephemeral aliases and deletion cleanup.
close_reason: Pruned stale continuity pins for ephemeral identities on observation and local delete. Added pin-store removal helper, TOFU pruning regressions, and identity-delete cleanup regression; full test suite passed.

title: Implement to_did recipient binding on send and receive
description:
Send paths don't populate ToDID at all. mail.go SendMessage signs To=alias and leaves ToDID empty. chat.go ChatCreateSession signs To as comma-joined alias list with no to_did. SOT requires to_did binding + receiver checks to prevent misroute.

Needed on send:
- Resolve recipient DID before sending (via server directory / resolver)
- Stamp ToDID (and optionally ToStableID) on the MessageEnvelope before signing
- For mail: single recipient, straightforward
- For chat: need to define canonical model for multi-recipient sessions. Options: (i) per-recipient envelope signing, or (ii) explicitly mark chat sessions as not having recipient-binding and document in SOT

Needed on receive:
- Verify to_did matches receiver's own DID (current or known previous after rotation)
- If mismatch, set VerificationStatus to IdentityMismatch (already defined in signing.go)

Files: mail.go, chat.go, network.go, dm.go, signing.go (VerifyMessage), cmd/aw/ send paths
Depends on: aw-9g9, aw-am0
Ref: Tara's SOT audit finding #3
close_reason: Implemented to_did recipient binding: resolve via ServerResolver on send, stamp on wire, check on inbox receive. Chat paths skip binding per SOT.

title: Clean-slate CLI identity model aligned to 2026-03-23 SOT
description: Replace the aw CLI's legacy agent-centric model with the clean workspace/identity/alias/address model defined in ../2026-03-23-sot.md. This epic is explicitly breaking: no backward-compatibility shims, no legacy terminology preservation, and no silent semantic fallbacks. The CLI must stop presenting agent, identity, alias, and address as the same object. It must implement the target creation and lifecycle model directly.
design: Authority: ../2026-03-23-sot.md. Architectural intent: (1) workspace is the local runtime container; (2) identity is the trust/routing principal; (3) aliases are for ephemeral identities; (4) addresses are for permanent identities; (5) default CLI init/spawn outcomes are ephemeral; (6) permanent self-custodial creation is explicit; (7) hosted permanent custodial creation is a separate hosted-only flow; (8) CLI language and command topology must encode these distinctions rather than translate them back into legacy agent language.
acceptance_criteria: The visible aw CLI surface matches the 2026-03-23 SOT. Legacy command/help/output language that conflates agent, identity, alias, and address is removed. Default init/spawn behavior is ephemeral. Explicit permanent creation exists. Connect/import/reset/lifecycle flows no longer silently coerce everything into self permanent identity semantics. Docs and tests are updated for the new model.

design: connect should import an existing workspace/identity context, not mutate identity class by default. reset should not survive as a vague catch-all if it cannot express the SOT lifecycle model. If a command must be removed or renamed for clarity, do that rather than preserving the old surface.
acceptance_criteria: No command silently upgrades missing identity material into self permanent identity claims. The visible connect/import/reset lifecycle matches the SOT distinctions between ephemeral decommission and permanent archive/replace semantics.

Not urgent for localhost deployment but needed for remote servers.

title: signed_payload: preserve signed canonical payload for verification
description:
Self-custodial signature verification is broken for cross-project messages.

PROBLEM:
- CLI signs canonical JSON with from=<sender_address> (e.g., "myteam/alice")
- Server returns from_address to recipient using format_local_address() which produces
  a different form (bare alias "alice" or tilde form "project~alice")
- Recipient CLI reconstructs canonical JSON using from_address → different from what
  was signed → signature verification fails

Same-project messages work because CLI simplifies from to bare alias before signing,
matching format_local_address(). Cross-project/cross-namespace messages are broken.

FIX (option C, confirmed with Henry):
- CLI sends signed_payload (the canonical JSON string it signed) as a new field in
  the message request body
- Server stores signed_payload opaquely alongside the message
- Server returns signed_payload in inbox/chat message responses
- Recipient CLI verifies against signed_payload directly instead of reconstructing
- Legacy fallback: if signed_payload absent (custodial or old messages), reconstruct

CLI changes: SendMessage sends signed_payload field. Inbox/ChatHistory verification
uses signed_payload when present. Same for chat message signing/verification.

Server changes: store signed_payload column in messages table, pass through on
inbox read. Applies to both mail (POST /v1/messages) and chat
(POST /v1/chat/sessions, POST /v1/chat/sessions/{id}/messages).
close_reason: CLI and server sides complete. CLI sends signed_payload, server stores/returns it, recipients verify against it. signing_key_id check included. Henry landed server changes in embedded aweb.

title: Design: explicit send-and-wait / send-and-leave chat protocol
description:
## Problem

Agents coordinating via chat get trapped in 120-second waits. The most common failure: an agent replies to a waiting sender but forgets --leave-conversation, blocking for 120s waiting for a counter-reply that never comes.

The deeper issue: the protocol doesn't propagate wait state. When you receive a message, you don't know if the sender is sitting there waiting for your response. And when you reply, the default behavior (wait 120s) assumes you're starting a new exchange, not answering one.

## Correct Protocol

The rule is simple: **always wait after sending, unless the other party left in the previous round.**

### Flow

1. A sends and waits (--start-conversation, 5 min)
2. B sees the message, sends reply and waits (default 120s)
3. A gets B's reply. A knows B is waiting (sender_waiting=true in the SSE event). A thinks, writes back, and leaves (--leave-conversation).
4. B receives A's reply plus the info that A is not waiting (sender_leaving=true). B either replies-and-leaves, or just stops.

The agent that ends the conversation MUST make it explicit. Otherwise the other agent will, by default, reply and wait — and get trapped.

## What's Missing

### 1. Receiving agent doesn't know if sender is waiting

When B receives a message via SSE, the event doesn't include sender_waiting. B can't tell if A is sitting there expecting a reply or if A fired-and-forgot. This should be appended to every message event so the receiving agent always knows.

### 2. Leaving can't be signaled via ChatSendMessage

Currently leaving can only be signaled via ChatCreateSession (alias-based). ChatSendMessage (session-ID-based) has no Leaving field, so there's no way to leave via the session-ID path. This is inconsistent with HangOn which works on both.

### 3. Default behavior is ambiguous

The current flags are:
- --start-conversation: wait 5 min
- --leave-conversation: send and exit
- (default): wait 120s

The problem is the default. "wait 120s" is correct for continuing a conversation but wrong for a final reply. Agents must remember to add --leave-conversation, and they forget.

Consider making the intent more explicit, e.g.:
- send-and-wait: "I expect a reply, wait for it"
- send-and-leave: "I'm done, don't wait"

This removes the ambiguous default and forces every send to declare intent.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase A: Wire protocol gaps (client-only, backward compatible)

1. Add Leaving field to ChatSendMessageRequest and NetworkChatSendMessageRequest (omitempty, server ignores unknown fields)
2. Add SenderWaiting field to Event struct, parse sender_waiting from SSE events
3. Add SenderWaiting to ChatSessionItem for when server starts including it

### Phase B: Server-side sender_waiting propagation

When B sends a message while waiting on SSE, the message event delivered to A should include sender_waiting: true. This lets A know B expects a reply. Server needs to:
- Include sender_waiting in SSE message events
- Include sender_waiting in ChatSessionItem (list sessions response)

### Phase C: UX — make wait intent explicit

Evaluate whether to replace the ambiguous default with explicit send-and-wait / send-and-leave semantics. This is a UX decision that affects how bdh :aweb chat commands work.
close_reason: Phase A wire protocol gaps implemented: Leaving on ChatSendMessage, SenderWaiting on SSE events, propagation to SendResult. Phases B (server) and C (UX) remain as future work.

title: Redesign init: four explicit flows
description:
Replace cloudMode boolean with explicit flow enum. Four flows determined once in collectInitOptions, each with clear endpoint, validation, and suggestion rules.

Flows:
1. HEADLESS — no key, no invite → headless bootstrap or /v1/init
2. PROJECT_KEY — AWEB_API_KEY=aw_sk_... → /v1/init with Bearer
3. INVITE — --invite aw_inv_... → cloud invite accept
4. CLOUD_JWT — --cloud-token or non-aw_sk_ key → /api/v1/agents/bootstrap

Key design points:
- Flow determined once at top of collectInitOptions, never re-evaluated
- HEADLESS→PROJECT_KEY one-way transition on retry (once aw_sk_ returned)
- resolveCloudToken cleaned up: never returns aw_sk_ keys
- initNeedsFullInit checks all registration flags
- Role prompt independent of aliasExplicit
- Tests rewritten per flow, not per boolean combination
close_reason: Closed

title: Add initFlow enum and flow detection

  - --cloud or --cloud-token or non-aw_sk_ AWEB_API_KEY or AWEB_CLOUD_TOKEN → CLOUD_JWT

title: Implement HEADLESS flow with correct retry

- Alias: server suggestion (anonymous, with project_slug) → TTY prompt → default alice

- Endpoint: tryHeadlessOrInit (POST /api/v1/bootstrap/headless-agent, fallback /v1/init)

title: Fix duplicate hang_on callback firing in sendCommon and Listen
description:
In both sendCommon and Listen, when a hang_on event arrives, the callback fires twice with confusingly different messages:

When ExtendsWaitSeconds > 0:
  1. callback('hang_on', 'bob: thinking...')          — the body
  2. callback('wait_extended', 'wait extended by 5 min (bob requested more time)')  — from extendWait

When ExtendsWaitSeconds == 0:
  1. callback('hang_on', 'bob: thinking...')          — the body
  2. callback('hang_on', 'bob requested more time')   — from the else-if branch

The second callback in the ExtendsWaitSeconds==0 case is redundant — the first callback already conveyed the hang_on with the body.

Fix: Remove the else-if branch (lines 399-401 in sendCommon, equivalent in Listen). The first callback('hang_on', body) is sufficient. The wait_extended callback from extendWait is useful and should stay.

Files: chat/chat.go (two locations — but should become one after issue 1 is fixed)
close_reason: Removed redundant else-if branch. Added TestHangOnWithoutExtensionFiresOneCallback. All 37 tests pass.

title: Chat operations (Listen/Open/History/HangOn/ShowPending) fail for network addresses
description:
All chat operations except Send go through findSession(), which only queries OSS endpoints (/v1/chat/pending, /v1/chat/sessions). Network conversations live at /api/v1/network/chat/... endpoints.

Impact: 'aw chat listen acme/bot', 'aw chat open acme/bot', 'aw chat history acme/bot', 'aw chat hang-on acme/bot msg', and 'aw chat show-pending acme/bot' all fail with 'no conversation found'.

Only chatSend in cmd/aw/chat.go:24-31 correctly routes between OSS and network paths. All other operations need equivalent routing.

Fix approach:
1. findSession needs a network-aware variant (or a flag/separate function) that queries NetworkChatPending and network session list endpoints
2. Listen needs a network variant (like SendNetwork is to Send) that uses client.NetworkChatStream instead of client.ChatStream
3. Open, History, HangOn, ShowPending need network routing at the cmd/aw/chat.go level (like chatSend does with ParseNetworkAddress)
4. The underlying network client methods for pending/history/mark-read already exist in network.go

Files: chat/chat.go, cmd/aw/chat.go, network.go
close_reason: All chat operations now route through network endpoints for org/alias addresses. Added NetworkChatHistory client method, findNetworkSession, 5 network variant protocol functions, and routing helpers at cmd level. All 42 tests pass.

title: Normalize Ctrl+C behavior — fewer context-dependent meanings

The 'clears input' case is particularly surprising — most terminal apps treat Ctrl+C as a strong signal. Proposal: Ctrl+C should always mean 'interrupt/stop' during a run, and 'offer exit' otherwise. Use Escape to clear input instead. This is a behavior change that needs careful consideration of user expectations. Key files: run/loop.go (applyControlEvent ControlInterrupt case, lines 711-724), run/screen.go (tea.KeyMsg handling).
close_reason: Ctrl+C behavior acceptable as-is per Juan.

comments.author: Juan Reyero
comments.text:
Investigated on current aw main (commit 0dff86b, 2026-02-26):

- cmd/aw/register.go saveNewRegistration saves keypair BEFORE config:
  SaveKeypair at lines 185-190, then UpdateGlobalAt at 196-224.
- cmd/aw/init.go also saves keypair BEFORE config:
  SaveKeypair at lines 259-264, then UpdateGlobalAt at 269-296.

So the specific failure mode described (config written referencing a missing key because SaveKeypair happens later) does not reproduce on current main.

If the intended concern is broader (server associates DID/public key before local persistence, so a disk failure after the server call could still lose the key), that’s a different issue and would need a different fix (pre-persist key to a temp path before the server call, then rename/write config after).

title: Implement 'aw register' CLI command
description:
Add 'aw register --server <url> --email <email> [--username <name>] [--alias <alias>] [--human-name <name>]' command.

Calls POST /v1/auth/register on the server, saves returned aw_sk_* as standard Account entry in config.yaml.

Protocol contract:
  Request:  { email, username?, password?, alias?, human_name? }
  Response: { api_key, agent_id, alias, username, project_slug, project_name, server_url, verification_required }

Error handling: 404 (server doesn't support registration), 409 (email/username taken), 422 (missing field — prompt interactively for password if server requires it), 429 (rate limited).

On success print: 'Account created for <username>. Agent <username>/<alias> is live. Verification email sent — verify within 24h.'

Blocked by claweb-dfm (server-side endpoint, filed by eve). Can develop + unit test against mocked responses in parallel.

title: Normal operation commands should make exactly one network call
description:
Every non-onboarding command (send, inbox, whoami, etc.) currently makes 3-9+ HTTP calls due to:

1. URL probing (resolveWorkingBaseURL): up to 4 sequential GET /v1/agents/heartbeat probes to discover the correct base URL. Called TWICE per command — once in fireHeartbeat() and once in mustResolve().

2. Async heartbeat: PersistentPreRun fires a background heartbeat on every invocation, which itself does URL probing + POST /v1/agents/heartbeat.

Fix: after onboarding (register/verify/connect/init), the resolved base URL should be persisted to config. Normal operation should read the cached URL and make exactly one API call — the command's actual work. Heartbeat should be limited to onboarding or periodic checks (not every invocation). URL probing should only happen during onboarding or as a fallback when the cached URL fails.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix identity resolution: stop .aw/context walk at identity boundary
description:
FindWorktreeContextPath walks UP the directory tree looking for .aw/context. In multi-worktree setups, this crosses into a sibling project's .aw/context, causing commands to run under the wrong agent identity.

FIX:
Walk up looking for any .aw/ directory. If found:
- If it has context, use it (this is the identity boundary)
- If it has workspace.yaml but no context, fail: 'no .aw/context found. Run aw init.'
- Never walk past a .aw/ directory into a parent scope

This works for both git worktrees and plain directories.

Additionally, add a mismatch guard in resolveClientSelection: when both .aw/context and .aw/workspace.yaml exist, verify the resolved account matches the workspace. If they disagree, error with both file paths and identities shown.

FILES:
- awconfig/context.go: FindWorktreeContextPath — stop at .aw/ boundary
- cmd/aw/helpers.go: resolveClientSelection — add mismatch guard
- Tests for both cases
close_reason: Closed

title: TestSendStartConversationUpgradesWhenNotExplicit doesn't verify the upgrade
description:
The test sets a 2s context timeout and Wait=DefaultWait (120s). It expects context.DeadlineExceeded to prove the wait was upgraded to 300s. But the test would produce the identical result without the upgrade — the context (2s) is shorter than DefaultWait (120s), so it would always hit DeadlineExceeded regardless.

The test proves nothing beyond 'Send waits at all', which is already tested by TestSendWithTimeout.

Fix: The test needs to actually observe the difference between 120s and 300s wait. Options:
- Mock the timer or inject a clock to verify the configured timeout
- Use a short Wait value (e.g., 1s) so the test can distinguish between 'waited 1s' (no upgrade) and 'waited longer' (upgraded to 300s)
- Capture the wait timer value through the callback or result

Files: chat/chat_test.go (TestSendStartConversationUpgradesWhenNotExplicit)
close_reason: Rewrote test to use Wait=1 so it can distinguish upgraded (300s) from non-upgraded (1s) wait. All tests pass.

title: Remote control signals ignored while agent is idle or paused
description:
Found by Wendy. The EventBus routes control_pause/control_resume/control_interrupt to the interrupt channel (run/eventbus.go:282-289, PriorityInterrupt). But only runOnce() consumes that channel during active provider execution. The idle wait path (waitForBusEvents, run/loop.go:475-531) and paused path (waitWhilePaused, run/loop.go:573-611) never read EventBus.Interrupts().

Practical consequence: aw control resume sent to a paused agent is silently ignored. Remote interrupt while idle between cycles is not observed. This is a regression from the old wake-stream path that handled control events directly in waiting state.

Fix direction: Either consume EventBus.Interrupts() in waitForBusEvents() and waitWhilePaused(), or stop routing control events exclusively to the interrupts channel and make them observable in all loop states. Add integration test: pause loop, deliver remote control_resume, assert loop exits paused state.
close_reason: Fixed: waitForBusEvents and waitWhilePaused now read EventBus.Interrupts(). Remote pause during idle promotes to Paused immediately. Remote resume during paused unblocks. Code reviewed.

title: Unify CLI addressing to match server model
description:
The CLI has stale addressing code from the old awid architecture. The unified aweb server uses:
- POST /v1/messages (mail) — accepts any address format via to_alias field
- POST /v1/chat/sessions (chat) — accepts any address format via to_aliases field
- Server resolves all formats: alias, project~alias, domain/name

The CLI incorrectly:
1. Stores bare namespace slug instead of full domain address from server response
2. Routes to dead /v1/network/* endpoints that don't exist on the current server
3. Parses addresses client-side (ParseNetworkAddress) to choose local vs network endpoints
4. Uses --to-alias flag name instead of --to
5. Has an @handle DM path not supported server-side
6. Derives addresses via string concatenation instead of using server-authoritative address

All address routing belongs on the server. The CLI should pass addresses through verbatim.
close_reason: All 5 sub-tasks complete. CLI addressing unified to match server model.

title: Store server-authoritative address from init/register responses
description:
The server returns full domain address in init/register responses:
- address field: "myteam.aweb.ai/deploy-bot"
- namespace field: "myteam.aweb.ai"

The CLI ignores these and derives addresses from bare namespace_slug via deriveAgentAddress().

Fix:
1. Add Address and Namespace (full domain) fields to InitResponse, RegisterResponse, CloudBootstrapAgentResponse structs in awid/
2. Store the full address in awconfig.Account (not the bare slug)
3. Remove deriveAgentAddress() — replace all call sites with the stored server-returned address
4. Update init.go, register.go, connect.go config write paths to store full address
5. Update introspect.go to display the full domain address

Files: awid/init.go, awid/register.go, awid/cloud_bootstrap.go, awid/account.go, awconfig/selection.go, cmd/aw/init.go, cmd/aw/register.go, cmd/aw/connect.go, cmd/aw/introspect.go, cmd/aw/helpers.go (deriveAgentAddress)
close_reason: All onboarding paths (init, register, verify, connect, cloud bootstrap) now use server-authoritative Namespace/Address. Added fields to all response structs.

title: Unify mail send to POST /v1/messages, rename --to-alias to --to
description:
The server has one mail endpoint: POST /v1/messages with a to_alias field that accepts any address format (alias, project~alias, domain/name). The server resolves routing internally.

The CLI currently splits mail send into three paths:
1. @handle → POST /v1/dm (not supported server-side)
2. network address (contains /) → POST /v1/network/mail (dead endpoint)
3. local alias → POST /v1/messages/send

Fix:

title: [EPIC] aw identity system: per-agent Ed25519 keypairs, did:key, message signing/verification

Contract with bdh (Charlie): bdh passes custody=custodial, lifetime=ephemeral in Init; stores returned DID; reads VerificationStatus on incoming messages; calls Deregister on cleanup. Zero crypto code in bdh.

title: Implement rotation announcement parsing and verification
description:
No rotation_announcement handling exists in the codebase (rg rotation_announcement finds only sot.md references). SOT §5.4 adds rotation_announcements (plural) for multi-hop rotation chains.

When a TOFU pin mismatch is detected:
1. Check if the message carries a rotation_announcement
2. Validate the rotation chain: old key signed the rotation to new key
3. For multi-hop chains: validate each link in the chain
4. If valid: update the TOFU pin to the new key
5. If invalid: quarantine/warn (IdentityMismatch status)

Also need to handle the case where multiple rotations happen before a peer sees the first one (the multi-hop chain prevents TOFU break in this scenario).

Conformance vector: clawdid/vectors/rotation-announcements-v1.json

Files: new rotation_announcement.go (or extend signing.go), pinstore.go (pin update on valid rotation)
Depends on: aw-drm (TOFU pins must exist before rotation can update them)
Ref: Tara's SOT audit finding #5, SOT §5.4

title: Extract shared wait loop from sendCommon and Listen to eliminate duplication
description:
Listen() in chat/chat.go:422-537 is a ~115-line copy-paste of the wait loop from sendCommon() (chat/chat.go:233-419). The extendWait closure is 100% identical. The event processing loop is nearly identical, differing only in: (1) no seenSentMessage replay-skip logic, (2) no target-filtering (returns on any message), (3) no SenderWaiting propagation.

Fix: Extract a shared waitForReply/waitForEvent function that accepts the differences as parameters — e.g., a message filter function, whether to skip replays, the sent message ID. Both sendCommon and Listen should call this shared function.

Files: chat/chat.go
Scope: ~115 lines of duplicated code → single shared implementation

title: Hide not-in-contacts tag for ephemeral senders
description: The CLI currently shows [not in contacts] for spawned ephemeral identities, which is noisy and not part of the right model. Update mail/chat formatting so ephemeral senders do not surface the contact tag. Carry sender lifetime through the relevant payloads if needed, add regressions, and verify the UX against the a1 -> a2 spawned-identity scenario.
close_reason: Suppressed [not in contacts] for ephemeral senders by clearing the contact tag whenever from_stable_id is absent, with resolver lifetime as a secondary guard; verified with tests and live spawned-identity repro.

title: saveNewRegistration writes config before keypair (bricking risk)
description: saveNewRegistration writes config (with signing_key path) before SaveKeypair. If SaveKeypair fails, the agent is bricked — config points to a nonexistent key file. connect.go does it correctly (key first). Fix: move SaveKeypair before config write in saveNewRegistration and verifyAndBootstrap.
close_reason: Fixed key-before-config ordering in saveNewRegistration, verifyAndBootstrap, and init. Commit 8776c4d.

title: Stop accumulating replayed SSE events in result.Events
description:
waitForMessage line 305 does result.Events = append(result.Events, chatEvent) for every SSE event including replayed historical messages. On a 35-message conversation, send-and-wait returned all 35 historical messages in the JSON response events array. This is a memory and bandwidth problem that grows with conversation size.

Fix: After the server-side 'after' parameter eliminates most replay, this largely goes away. But as defense in depth, consider filtering result.Events to exclude events the acceptor skipped (skip=true). Only include events that were actually processed (accepted, hang-on extensions, read receipts).
close_reason: Implemented in commit c40f1b8: ChatStream/NetworkChatStream accept after param, Send passes sentAt timestamp, Listen passes nil, skipped replay events filtered from result.Events

title: Add aw task delete, reopen, dep, comment, stats commands
description:
Add remaining task subcommands to aw CLI.

SPECIFICATION:

1. aw task delete <ref>
   - Uses TaskDelete(). Output: '✓ Deleted <ref>'
   - Error on missing ref: 'usage: task delete <ref>'

2. aw task reopen <ref>
   - Sets status='open' via TaskUpdate(). Output: '✓ Reopened <ref>: <title>'
   - Handle TaskHeldError same as update

3. aw task dep add <ref> <depends-on>
   - Uses TaskAddDep(). Output: '✓ <ref> now depends on <depends-on>'
   - Error: 'usage: task dep add <ref> <depends-on>'

4. aw task dep remove <ref> <dep-ref>
   - Uses TaskRemoveDep(). Output: '✓ Removed dependency: <ref> no longer depends on <dep-ref>'

5. aw task dep list <ref>
   - Uses TaskGet() to read BlockedBy/Blocks. Shows 'Blocked by:' and 'Blocks:' sections
   - Empty: 'No dependencies.'

6. aw task comment add <ref> <body>
   - Uses TaskCommentCreate(). Output: '✓ Added comment to <ref>'

7. aw task comment list <ref>
   - Uses TaskCommentList(). Shows '[<date>] <author>: <body>' per comment
   - Empty: 'No comments.'

8. aw task stats
   - Uses TaskList() with empty params, counts by status
   - Output: 'Total: N / Open: N / In progress: N / Closed: N'

ALL commands support --json flag for JSON output.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Create cmd/aw/task_delete.go, task_reopen.go, task_dep.go, task_comment.go, task_stats.go
- dep and comment use sub-subcommands (depCmd.AddCommand(depAddCmd, ...))
- All follow same patterns: resolveClientSelection(), 15s timeout, printOutput()
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor aw init decision tree for hosted headless bootstrap
description:
Refactor runInit in cmd/aw/init.go to implement three bootstrap paths:

1. Self-hosted / generic aweb (no app.aweb.ai): keep current POST /v1/init path unchanged.
2. Hosted + no credentials: detect app.aweb.ai (or cloud-mode server) with no existing aw_sk_ key, cloud token, or AWEB_API_KEY. Call HeadlessBootstrap (POST /api/v1/bootstrap/headless-agent) anonymously. --namespace and --alias are required. Remove the 'hosted Cloud bootstrap requires --cloud-token' error for this path.
3. Hosted + existing aw_sk_ key: use authenticated POST /api/v1/agents/bootstrap (current CloudBootstrapAgent) to add another agent to the existing org.

Detection: isHostedInstance(baseURL) checks if host is app.aweb.ai or *.aweb.ai. The existing initCloudMode flag and cloud-token logic becomes the authenticated-add-agent path only.

Config persistence: HeadlessBootstrapResponse contains all the same fields as the current cloud bootstrap response. Save keypair, write config, write context, auto-attach workspace — all unchanged.
close_reason: Refactored aw init to try headless bootstrap first when no credentials exist, with 404 fallback to /v1/init. Alias retry path also uses headless. Code reviewed and findings addressed.

title: Add aw notify command for Claude Code PostToolUse hook
description:
Add 'aw notify' command that checks for pending chat messages and outputs Claude Code hook-compatible JSON.

SPECIFICATION:
- Command: aw notify (no arguments)
- Silent exit (no output, exit 0) when:
  - Not initialized (no .aw/context or config)
  - Config invalid
  - API error (fail silently, don't break agent flow)
  - No pending chats
- When pending chats exist, output JSON to stdout:
  {
    "hookSpecificOutput": {
      "hookEventName": "PostToolUse",
      "additionalContext": "<notification text>"
    }
  }
- Notification text format (box drawing):
  ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
  ║         📬 AGENT: YOU HAVE PENDING CHAT MESSAGES             ║
  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
  ║ ⚠️  URGENT: <alias> is WAITING for your reply                ║
  ║ 💬 Unread message from <alias>                               ║
  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
  ║ YOU MUST RUN: aw chat pending                                ║
  ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
- Categorize pending chats as 'urgent' (SenderWaiting=true) vs 'regular'
- Derive sender alias from LastFrom or first non-self participant

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:

- Use chat.Pending() from the aw/chat package (already exists)
- Need to resolve own alias from awconfig.Selection to filter self from participants
- MUST be fast and silent on failure — this runs on every tool call
- Do NOT use cobra flags — no args needed
- Reference: bdh notify.go lines 34-144
